ItacPersonItem.Notes: Was interpreter for Arabic, Turkish, and Persian at the Moroccan court in Fez. Professor of Arabic in Alcala` (Spain) in 1591. Joined the Lincei in Naples in 1612. See Fernando Rodríguez Mediano, ‘Diego De Urrea en Italia’, in Al-Qantara, XXV, 1, 2004, pp. 183-201.
ItacPersonItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
